rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (noun) / ceremony — hard ban
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Line 49 'what you don't practice yourself' is the verb form, not the banned noun; AE101 M3 noun-ban not tripped.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
Register match.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: AE101 quintet holds: Boris-direct on the skill primitive, Martin frame-move on the curate-vs-author ratio, Godin peer-warmth, Risto-forward close ('Go.') — appropriate for an M3 meta-frame primer. No author-we, no atmospheric misfire, no negation self-contradiction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Rough-cut drafts for articles.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Article-drafting rule; target is a curriculum lecture.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit original when paraphrasing named technique.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Pocock's grill-me credited (line 12); STRIDE to Kohnfelder/Garg + Shostack (line 18); Saltzer & Schroeder for least-privilege (line 18). No creator-name, no author-we in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body uses second person throughout; line 24 'the training authors everything' is the sanctioned impersonal-training-as-subject recast, not 'the student'. No trainer-delivery mechanics above the cut.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No self-compound/substrate/three-pattern leak. 'frontier practitioner moves are curated for you' (line 30) is the lecture's stated design rationale, framed and earned in-beat, not an unprimed maintainer term.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Over-hedge detector.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No reassurance-shaped sentences. 'Curating it would be theatre' (line 22) is a sharp dismissal of the alternative, not a defense of a choice.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verbatim-frame-cite in reference artefact.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Reference-artefact capture discipline; target is a lecture, not a frame-capture reference.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Each practitioner (Pocock, Kohnfelder/Garg, Shostack, Saltzer & Schroeder) named once in the lecture body; no credit-collecting repetition.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Punchy framing keeps punch; carve-out lives downstream.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'Two curated, one authored' and 'Don't make general what you don't practice yourself' land as slogans and are followed by concrete rationale paragraphs; no hedged-middle softening needed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project strategy docs, NOT student-facing curriculum body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No 'vendor' in body, no 'not an aspirational X' / 'this isn't marketing' / 'the real practitioners' defensive phrasing. 'Not a platform-team design document' (line 26) is contrastive teaching about how kits grow, not vendor-defense.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: ADR (lines 37, 45) appears unexpanded but the audience is software-engineer ICs and the term lands operationally inside this same module's Exercise 2 (threat-model-with-stride.md Phase 3 'write the ADR'). No premature LLM-internal vocabulary planted.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audit closer when trimming body-prose forcing function.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture closes on 'Go.' after a principle line; no dangling demonstratives, no bypass-the-agent punchline.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Past tense for exercise recap sections — heading and body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture has no exercise-recap / 'What happened' section; recap-tense rule does not apply.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Human-to-agent verbs stay peer-flat: 'invoke', 'author... in conversation with', 'push back', 'fed it your system'. No bark/order/command/whip.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Frames what the student produces ('three things your staff engineer and your CISO would actually read'); no 'you already think' / 'you're missing'. No ability-tier pegging.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'skill' earned in 'What's a skill, one breath' (lines 10-12). STRIDE primed by 'threat modeling' (line 6). ADR unexpanded but lands operationally in this module's Ex2; audience = engineers. Bare M1/M2/M3 fine at M3.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban (Agents 101 default).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Agents-101-default business-audience word ban; AE101 audience is software-engineer ICs, so SVP-marketing-flinch test does not gate this file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No memory/context conflation; 'context' not used in the runtime-state sense in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Golden path only in body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No troubleshooting / 'what if X fails' branches in body; recovery guidance absent.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Trust Claude's defaults for structure.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prompt-shape rule; lecture body contains no fenced prompt with output destination.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module. Compound-shape varies per module — never repeat same Debrief.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Debrief / compound-shape rule; this is a lecture with no Debrief section.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle another module's voice.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Voice matches M3 'earned trust' mood; no M1-joyful-creation or M5-long-running tone bleed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't ask student to manually diff or spot-check Claude's output as error-catching.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Ex1/Ex2 watch-fors ask the student to JUDGE the skill's output (decide what it got right/missed) — domain judgement, not mechanical error-catching diff.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Simulation personas never alone.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Self-study simulation rule; lecture is in-cohort body with no simulation persona.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No "What research says" callout boxes
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Attributions are inline one-liners (Pocock, STRIDE history, Intercom data); no consultancy-deck callout box.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student moves stay in conversation with agent.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All student moves stay in conversation with Claude (invoke, author through conversation, push back); no editor/terminal forcing function in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Layer named where referenced: 'CLAUDE.local.md (personal, gitignored)' (line 26); skill homes given as personal '~/.claude/skills/...' vs team '.claude/skills/' explicitly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body grep for em-dash returned zero in lines 1-52; hook auto-fix surface clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Section headers use command verbs when section is about doing something.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture-register declarative noun-phrase headers ('What's a skill, one breath', 'Why this proportion matters') are explicitly permitted for understanding-not-doing reader job.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use target's title, never filename.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No markdown cross-doc links in body; code-span paths present are skill/layer homes (.claude/skills/, CLAUDE.local.md), not link references.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'skill' collision handled: line 12 defines the Claude Code skill primitive and its SKILL.md home; lines 26/47 distinguish personal vs team-kit skill homes and curated-vs-authored. Disambiguation present at first authored-write framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body Claude:agent is 7:1, but the topic is authoring-through-conversation (invocation/addressee, correctly 'Claude') plus Claude Code UI surface; 'the agent can invoke' (line 12) is correct agent-as-actor. Not the 10:0 assistant-shaped signal.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'on a Tuesday afternoon' (line 8) / 'on a Tuesday' (line 30) are idioms for an ordinary working day, not session-relative cadence anchors (no overnight/tomorrow/morning-after gap claim). No module-cadence assumption smuggled.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats stay invisible in flow.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No 'Pause before', 'Meta-moment', 'Step back', or wellness-coach contemplative prefixes; transitions carry their own command/action.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No meta-ask when student has already decided.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prompt-construction rule (Claude asking student what they already decided); lecture body has no such meta-loop prompt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: `.md` is default file type for student artifacts across curriculum.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No 'Save it as <filename>' artifact prompt in this lecture; .md default not exercised here.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cut NVA: true but not moving student forward.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: History (STRIDE 1999, Saltzer & Schroeder 1975) and Intercom figures earn their place as voice-one credibility for the curate-vs-author thesis; not detached trivia. Watch-fors stay above the cut as student-relevant orientation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body "read it" / "look at it" / "notice X" after chat-print = dead weight.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No chat-print-then-'read-it' narration in lecture body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forward-callout closer: student as grammatical subject.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Forward gestures keep student as subject ('you invoke two more', 'by the end of 1h45 you'll have three things'). 'the training authors everything' (line 24) is method-description, not a training-as-subject value-prop closer; no role-gating to a non-inhabited role.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Operational reminders live at moment of use, not only where introduced.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Per-module operational-setting reminder rule (thinking-effort, model, worktree path) targets module-file/exercise tops; a primer lecture carries no operational setting to restate.
